enonce.texte: Instructions: Match each media source to its correct description. (Relie chaque source d'information à sa description correcte.)
images: A picture of a radio with sound waves coming out.
A picture of a television showing news.
A picture of a newspaper with articles.
A picture of a computer screen showing news online.

questions.texte: You can listen to news on this device.
You can watch news with pictures and sound on this device.
You can read news in print on paper.
You can read and watch news on the internet.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Complete the sentences with the correct word from the box. (Complète les phrases avec le mot correct de la boîte.)

Word box: headline, article, news bar, caption, breaking news, current issues.

questions.texte: The big title of a news story is the ______.
A short text under a picture is a ______.
A ______ is a short piece of writing about a news topic.
Very important news that happens suddenly is ______.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the short article and answer the questions below. (Lis le court article et réponds aux questions ci-dessous.)

Article:

Headline: 'School Celebrates 10th Anniversary'

The school held a big celebration on Saturday. Students, teachers, and parents attended the event. There were songs, dances, and speeches. The headmaster said it was a great day for the school. Everyone was very happy.
images: A picture of children singing and dancing at a school celebration.

questions.texte: What is the headline of the article?
When did the celebration take place?
Who attended the event?
What did the headmaster say?
questions.reponse: It was a great day for the school.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Read the description and identify the news source. (Lis la description et identifie la source d'information.)

questions.texte: You hear the news on a device with speakers. You can listen while you are walking or driving.
You see the news on a screen with pictures and videos. You can watch it at home.
You read the news on paper. You can buy it at a shop or someone brings it to your home.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the news bar and answer the questions. (Lis le bandeau d'actualités et réponds aux questions.)

News Bar:

'BREAKING NEWS: President visits flooded region. People receive aid. Government promises to help rebuild homes.'

questions.texte: What is the news about?
questions.reponse: The President visiting a flooded region.

questions.texte: What are the people receiving?
What does the government promise to do?

enonce.texte: Instructions: Write a news headline for each situation below. (Écris un titre de journal pour chaque situation ci-dessous.)
images: A picture of a school football team winning a match.
A picture of a community cleaning a river.

questions.texte: Situation: Your school football team won the championship.
questions.reponse: School Wins Championship! or School Team Crowned Champions!

questions.texte: Situation: The community cleaned the river on Saturday.
questions.reponse: Community Cleans River! or River Cleanup a Success!

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the short article and identify the parts. (Lis le court article et identifie les parties.)

Article:

Headline: 'New Hospital Opens in Town'

Caption: 'The new hospital will serve over 50,000 people.'

Article: A new hospital opened on Monday. It is in the center of town. The hospital has 100 beds and 50 doctors. People from the community came to celebrate the opening. The mayor said it is good for the town.
images: A picture of a new hospital building with people celebrating outside.

questions.texte: What is the headline of the article?
What is the caption?
questions.reponse: The new hospital will serve over 50,000 people.

questions.texte: How many beds does the hospital have?
What did the mayor say?
questions.reponse: The mayor said it is good for the town.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Read the statements and decide if they are True (T) or False (F). (Lis les affirmations et décide si elles sont Vraies (V) ou Fausses (F).)

questions.texte: A newspaper article has a headline.
You can only read news on television.
A caption is a short text under a picture.
Breaking news is not very important.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Create a news bar (2-3 sentences) about a current issue in your school or community. Use the vocabulary from the lesson. (Crée un bandeau d'actualités (2-3 phrases) sur un sujet d'actualité dans ton école ou ta communauté. Utilise le vocabulaire de la leçon.)

questions.texte: Write a news bar about a current issue in your school or community.
questions.reponse: The school library is getting new books. Students can borrow books on Monday. The headmaster says reading is important.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Answer the following questions about news sources. (Réponds aux questions suivantes sur les sources d'information.)

questions.texte: Which news source do you prefer? Why? (radio, television, newspapers, or online news)
questions.reponse: I prefer television because I can see pictures and videos. I like watching the news with my family.

questions.texte: How do you get news about what is happening in Cameroon?
questions.reponse: I get news about Cameroon from the television and radio. My father also reads the newspaper and tells us the news.
